commit_msg: docs(casefiles): archive case files 004-007 — the pipeline confessions
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Ledger
subtitle: He filed the confession under 'docs' and called it case closure.
when_where: 🕐 1:24 PM. Still main. Still March 13th. 📚 The work was done—the pipeline was fixed, the arguments isolated, the paths explicit. Now came the part that separated the professional from the amateur: he documented it. He archived cases 004-007, the pipeline confessions, each one a breadcrumb trail leading straight back to the original sin.
who_whom: 🔍 Omkar and the case files. They were evidence now—four sealed documents that detailed how the pipeline had been broken and how he'd put it back together. 🗂️ By archiving them, he wasn't hiding the crime; he was canonizing it. The confessions would live forever in the git log, dated and attributed, impossible to deny.
what_why: 📝 He wrote it all down. Cases 004-007, the pipeline confessions, now archived under docs. 💀 It was the oldest trick in the book: you can't be convicted of something you admitted to in writing. 🧠 The documentation was both shield and sword—proof of competence and proof of culpability, depending on who was reading. 📊 By the time anyone looked back, the narrative would be his: not a man covering his tracks, but an engineer documenting a system's evolution.
verdict: He didn't solve the crime; he just made sure he'd be remembered as the one who confessed to it first.
